---
title: "Questions, methods, and scientific work in public"
page-layout: full
---

:::{.hero}
## Duojiang

This site is a concise home for research questions, project pages, publications, and reusable materials. It is designed to stay readable for collaborators, review committees, and curious visitors outside the field.

::: {.hero-meta}
::: {.hero-chip}
Focus
Scientific themes, outputs, and working notes
:::
::: {.hero-chip}
Format
Short pages, plain language, and room for figures
:::
::: {.hero-chip}
Update rhythm
Light edits during the year, deeper updates around papers and talks
:::
:::
:::

## Research at a glance

:::{.card-grid}
::: {.panel}
### Research themes

Use the research page to explain your scientific questions in plain language. Keep the number of themes small and make each one legible to non-specialists.
:::
::: {.panel}
### Publications

List papers, preprints, posters, and code releases together with one-sentence summaries that explain why each output matters.
:::
::: {.panel}
### Projects

Create one page per active line of work. A strong project page states the question, approach, current status, and related outputs.
:::
:::

## What this site is for

- Explain your work without assuming deep prior knowledge.
- Give collaborators a stable place to understand questions, methods, and outputs.
- Keep a public-facing record that grows steadily instead of becoming a backlog.

## A simple publishing rule

Put polished summaries here and keep supporting detail in linked papers, repositories, slides, or datasets. This keeps the site concise while still useful.
